body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: partial
irrlicht_observes: no

## Reasoning

The canonical primitive is a *checkpoint/branch* capability: rewind to an EARLIER checkpoint (one of several), restoring files and/or conversation, then continue cleanly from the rewound point. Aider 0.86.2 has no such primitive. Enumerating every slash command in the installed package (`aider/commands.py`, all `def cmd_*` methods) yields `/undo`, `/reset`, `/clear` — and NO `/rewind`, `/checkpoint`, or `/fork`. The aider docs confirm the same surface (https://aider.chat/docs/usage/commands.html).

What aider DOES have, and why this is `partial` rather than `no`:

- **`/undo`** — "Undo the last git commit if it was done by aider" (`raw_cmd_undo`). It reverts ONLY the single most-recent aider commit (it bails with `tool_error` if the last commit isn't in `self.coder.aider_commit_hashes`, if it was already pushed, or has >1 parent). This is a degenerate one-step case of the rewind primitive: it restores FILES to the previous commit, but does not touch conversation state and offers no multi-point picker.
- **`/reset`** — drops all files AND clears chat history. That is the `session-reset` neighbour (explicitly called out under "Not to be confused with"), not a rewind.
- **`/clear`** — clears chat history only. Also session-reset territory.

Because the closest match (`/undo`) covers only files, only the last commit, and only when aider made it, aider partially supports the primitive. There is no branch/fork and no arbitrary-checkpoint restore.

## Why irrlicht_observes is `no`

The aider adapter is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser}` (core/.../aider/agent.go). One markdown transcript per project CWD; there is NO session_id rotation and no second transcript file — so a "linked new session_id" (the fork branch of the scenario's observable signal) is structurally impossible for aider.

The in-place branch of the signal also produces no distinguishing event. Aider's `InputOutput.get_input()` logs EVERY raw line — including slash commands — to the chat history via `self.user_input(inp)` (io.py:733) before dispatching, so a `/undo` lands in the transcript as a `#### /undo` line. The irrlicht parser maps any `#### …` line to a `user_message` event that OPENS a turn (parser.go ParseLineRaw); the `/undo` success blockquote (`io.tool_output` → `> …`) is ignored as status; IdleFlush then synthesizes `turn_done`. Net effect: `/undo` surfaces as an ordinary `ready → working → ready` arc on the SAME session, identical in shape to a normal turn. The git HEAD movement is invisible (the adapter watches the markdown transcript, not the repo). The only trace that a rewind happened is the literal string `/undo` inside a `####` line — and the parser does not classify slash-command markers as a distinct lifecycle event. So none of the scenario's user-observable signals (rewound-state reflection, same-vs-new session_id distinction, a detectable rewind) are emitted as anything other than a vanilla turn.

## Capability key

The primitive maps to `checkpoint_rewind` in `capabilities.json`. Aider's `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` has no such key (and correctly so) — confirming the absence of a checkpoint primitive at the capability level.

## Matrix agreement

This ratifies the existing coverage cell (`agent_supports: partial`, `irrlicht_observes: no`) and the `scenarios.json` recipe scope note (`applicable: false`, recording deferred). No drift.
caveats: `/undo` is files-only and single-step: it reverts just the last aider-authored git commit (refuses if not aider's, already pushed, or a merge). It is NOT a multi-point checkpoint picker and does not restore conversation state — so even the supported slice is a narrow, degenerate case of the rewind primitive.

feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant: a `/undo` is observable only as the literal `#### /undo` text inside an otherwise normal ready→working→ready turn; irrlicht emits no rewind-specific lifecycle event and never sees the git HEAD movement, because the FilesUnderCWD adapter watches the markdown transcript, not the repo.

A forked/new-session_id rewind (the codex-style branch of the observable signal) is structurally impossible for aider: FilesUnderCWD binds one fixed transcript file per project CWD, so there is no second session row to link to.

sources.note: Command reference: /undo = 'Undo the last git commit if it was done by aider' (git-only, last commit); no /rewind, /checkpoint, or /fork documented
aider 0.86.2 full slash-command enumeration (def cmd_*): /undo, /reset, /clear present; no rewind/checkpoint/fork. raw_cmd_undo reverts only the last aider commit and bails otherwise

get_input() at line 733 logs every raw line (incl. slash commands) to .aider.chat.history.md via user_input(); tool_output() writes blockquote status lines — so /undo enters the transcript as a #### line
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser}: one fixed transcript per project CWD, no session_id rotation — a linked-new-session_id fork is structurally impossible

ParseLineRaw maps any '#### …' line to a turn-opening user_message; blockquotes are ignored status; IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done. /undo therefore yields a vanilla ready→working→ready arc indistinguishable from a normal turn
No checkpoint_rewind key — confirms aider has no checkpoint primitive at the capability level
